data.title: Western Alliance stock slides as bank expects NII decline in Q4; Q3 earnings miss
data.description:
Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) stock gapped down 5.8% in Thursday after-hours trading as the regional lender expects its net interest income to fall sequentially in Q4. And, even with continued momentum in loan and deposit growth, alongside an acceleration in NII, the bank delivered Q3 earnings that rose less than expected.

For the year, the Phoenix-based bank now expects 2024 NII to rise 10%-11%, compared with the previous view of 9%-14% growth and the +12.86% Visible Alpha consensus.

2024 noninterest income growth, though, was revised up to 27%-29% from 15%-25%, according to a slide presentation. That still trails the Visible Alpha estimate of 70.26%. Noninterest expense growth for the year is anticipated to be 14%-16%, vs. prior target of 9%-13% and Visible Alpha estimate of 20.14%.

It sees Q4 2024 NII retreating about 3% from Q3, a far weaker target than the Visible Alpha consensus of 17.46%. Q4 noninterest income is expected to grow 8%-12% (vs. 29.67% Visible Alpha consensus), and noninterest expense is forecast to fall 5%-9% (vs. +4.03% Visible Alpha consensus).

Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.80, falling short of the $1.89 average analyst estimate, rose from $1.75 in Q2 and slid from $1.97 a year before.

Net revenue of $823.1M, surpassing the $805.8M expected, advanced from $771.8M in the prior quarter and $716.2M in Q3 2023.

Net interest income accelerated to $696.9M from $656.6M in Q2 and $587.0M in Q3 of last year.

Noninterest income totaled $126.2M (vs. $116.5M Visible Alpha estimate), up from $115.2M in Q2 and down from $129.2M a year earlier. Noninterest expense of $537.4M (vs. $499.1M Visible Alpha estimate) increased from $486.8M in Q2 and $426.2M in Q3 2023.

Provision for credit losses was $33.6M compared with $37.1M in Q2 and $12.1M in the year-ago period.

Total deposits of $68.0B at Sept. 30 rose from $66.2B at June 30. Held-for-investment loans, net of deferred fees, of $53.3B at the end of Q3 compared with $52.4B at the end of Q2.

Conference call on Oct. 18 at 12:00 p.m. ET.

data.title: Crown Holdings Q3 results beat street view, raises FY24 guidance
data.description:
Shares of Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK) were driven higher in after-hours trading on the heels of better-than-expected Q3 results and raised guidance for FY24 that beat the Street view.

Increased shipments of beverage cans and food cans helped offset unfavorable foreign currency headwinds and lower volumes in the company’s other businesses, resulting in an increased adjusted profit of $1.99 per share on revenue of $3.07B, both of which beat expectations.

On an unadjusted basis, the company swung to a loss due to settlement charges related to the transfer of its U.S. and Canadian pension plan obligations. Including the $517M in settlement charges, Crown Holdings posted a loss of $1.47 versus a profit of $1.33 in the same quarter last year.

By region, net sales for its domestic beverage business increased by 5.6%, while European sales were up 6.9%, both of which offset a 7.5% decline in its Asian business and 5% drop in transit packaging.

For the full year, Crown Holdings (CCK) now expects to earn a profit of $6.25 to $6.35 per share, an increase from previous guidance of $6.00 to $6.25 per share and above the consensus estimate of $6.16.

For Q4, the company is guiding for a profit of $1.45 to $1.55 per share, which straddles the $1.53 consensus estimate.

data.title: Bausch rejects latest bondholder debt proposals - WSJ
data.description:
• Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) has rejected the latest round of debt-restructuring proposals from bondholders as it attempts to unload its stake in Bausch + Lomb (NYSE:BLCO) to raise cash.
  • Bondholders such as Apollo Global Management and GoldenTree Asset Management had made offers that seek to restructure debt without the need to file for bankruptcy, The Wall Street Journal reported.
  • Those creditors were also asking to exchange some debt for equity in Bausch + Lomb.
  • Bausch holds an 88% stake in Bausch + Lomb. Bausch also has ~$11B in debt maturing by 2028.

data.title: Western Alliance GAAP EPS of $1.80 misses by $0.09, revenue of $823.1M beats by $17.31M
data.description:
• Western Alliance press release (NYSE:WAL): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.80 misses by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $823.1M (+14.9% Y/Y) beats by $17.31M.

  • The Company had 3,426 full-time equivalent employees and 56 offices at September 30, 2024, compared to 3,310 full-time equivalent employees and 56 offices at June 30, 2024, and 3,272 full-time equivalent employees and 56 offices at September 30, 2023.

  • HFI loans, net of deferred fees totaled $53.3 billion at September 30, 2024, compared to $52.4 billion at June 30, 2024, and $49.4 billion at September 30, 2023.
  • Provision for credit losses totaled $33.6 million for the third quarter 2024, compared to $37.1 million for the second quarter 2024, and $12.1 million for the third quarter 2023.
